title: Whitesands Students Excel at Eleko Science Challenge
excerpt: Eight Whitesands students brought home gold at this year's National Science Olympiad, placing first in three categories including Biology, Chemistry, and Physics.
content:
Eight students from Whitesands School have made the school community proud by clinching gold at the National Science Olympiad held in Abuja. The team, coached by Mr. Emeka Okafor, placed first in Biology, Chemistry, and Physics categories, outcompeting over 120 schools from across Nigeria.

The achievement caps a season of intense preparation, early-morning laboratory sessions, and mentorship from senior faculty. Principal Mrs. Adaeze Nwosu praised the students' dedication and called it a testament to the school's commitment to academic excellence rooted in the motto Duc in Altum.

The winning students will be honoured at the end-of-term assembly and will represent Nigeria at the West Africa regional finals in March.

title: 25th Anniversary Gala: A Night of Gratitude and Vision
excerpt: Over 400 alumni, parents, staff, and friends gathered to celebrate 25 years of Whitesands School with an elegant evening of reflection and forward vision.
content:
The Whitesands School 25th Anniversary Gala was held on the school grounds on Saturday, 14 March 2026. The evening brought together alumni from across the globe, current families, and founding staff members in a celebration that honoured the school's legacy and cast a vision for its next chapter.

Highlights included a keynote address from class of 2005 alumnus Dr. Chisom Eze, now a cardiologist in London; a surprise reunion of the school's founding choir; and the unveiling of the new STEM Innovation Hub, funded by the Anniversary Campaign.

The Board of Governors announced a full scholarship fund in honour of the school's founder, to be awarded annually to one exceptional student from an underprivileged background.

title: New STEM Innovation Hub Opens Its Doors
excerpt: The newly built STEM Innovation Hub — featuring robotics labs, a 3D printing suite, and collaborative maker-spaces — is now open to all students.
content:
Whitesands School has officially opened its brand-new STEM Innovation Hub, a state-of-the-art facility designed to inspire curiosity and practical problem-solving in students from Year 7 through the Upper Sixth.

The hub houses three fully equipped robotics labs, a 3D printing and fabrication suite, a coding studio with 40 workstations, and a collaborative maker-space. It was built with funds raised during the 25th Anniversary Campaign and is expected to serve over 600 students each week.

Head of STEM Dr. Ifeoma Anieto described the hub as a game-changer: 'We can now offer students experiences that were once confined to universities. This is hands-on learning at its finest.'

title: Virtue of the Month: Perseverance — Stories from Our Students
excerpt: Students across the school share how they have practised perseverance this term — from overcoming exam anxiety to pushing through in sports training.
content:
Each month, Whitesands School spotlights a core virtue that shapes its community. This March, the virtue is Perseverance — and students have been living it loudly.

Year 10 student Adaora Chukwuemeka described staying up to rewrite her history essay three times until she was truly satisfied with it. Year 12 student Tobenna Nwosu shared how he pushed through a knee injury to complete his football season. Even younger students in Year 7 submitted drawings and journal entries showing what perseverance looks like in daily school life.

These stories were displayed in the school atrium and shared in the weekly assembly. They serve as a reminder that character is built in small, daily acts of determination.

title: Admissions Open for 2026/2027 Academic Year
excerpt: Whitesands School is now accepting applications for the 2026/2027 academic year. Entrance assessments will be held on 25 April and 9 May 2026.
content:
Whitesands School is pleased to announce that applications for the 2026/2027 academic year are now open. We are accepting candidates for Years 7, 10, and 12 entry points.

Entrance assessments are scheduled for Saturday 25 April and Saturday 9 May 2026, covering English, Mathematics, and a general aptitude paper. Shortlisted candidates will also be invited for a brief interview with the admissions team.

Families are encouraged to attend our Open Day on 19 April 2026, where prospective students can tour the campus, meet faculty, and ask questions. To register or learn more, visit the Admissions page or contact Mrs. Ngozi Eze at admissions@whitesandsschool.edu.ng.

title: Inter-House Sports Week: Eagles Clinch the Championship
excerpt: After five thrilling days of competition across track, field, and team sports, Eagles House emerged champions of the 2026 Inter-House Sports Week.
content:
The 2026 Inter-House Sports Week wrapped up last Friday in front of a packed school ground, with Eagles House lifting the championship trophy after a dominant performance across disciplines.

Eagles accumulated the highest points in athletics (track and field), football, and table tennis, edging out Lions in the final standings by a margin of 14 points. The week-long event was a showcase of school spirit, with students, parents, and staff cheering passionately from the stands.

Most Valuable Athlete awards went to Chukwudi Obi (Year 11, Eagles) for athletics and Amara Esimone (Year 9, Lions) for swimming. Sports Director Mr. Kelechi Nwosu commended all participants for their sportsmanship and energy.

title: Teacher of the Term: Mr. Emmanuel Obi Recognised for Excellence
excerpt: Mathematics teacher Mr. Emmanuel Obi has been named Teacher of the Term for his innovative classroom strategies and transformative impact on student confidence.
content:
Mr. Emmanuel Obi, who teaches Further Mathematics and Statistics to Years 11 and 12, has been named Whitesands School's Teacher of the Term for the second term of 2025/2026.

Nominated by both students and fellow staff, Mr. Obi was recognised for introducing a peer-tutoring model in Further Mathematics that has significantly lifted pass rates in the class, as well as for his weekly problem-solving club that draws students from multiple year groups.

'Mr. Obi makes you feel like you can actually do mathematics,' said Year 12 student Somto Ikenna. 'He turns the hardest topics into puzzles you actually want to solve.'

Mr. Obi accepted the recognition with characteristic humility, dedicating it to his students.
